title: Crispy Nutty Coconut Stir-fry
description: A quick and easy weeknight Argentinean dish featuring unsweetened coconut milk, sous-vide cooked nuts, and a touch of crispy texture to satisfy any craving.

ingredients.name: sliced almonds or peanuts (unsalted)
ingredients.notes: soaked and dried in the fridge for 2 hours before sous-vide cooking at 160°C for 45 minutes, then cooled to room temperature

ingredients.name: garlic paste or minced garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: about a teaspoon of finely chopped garlic

instructions: Preheat your sous-vide machine to 160°C and prepare the nuts by soaking them in water for about 2 hours, then drying them and cooking as per instructions.
In a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat, add the sesame oil. Once it's hot, add the sliced almonds (or peanuts) and stir-fry until lightly golden brown, about 3-5 minutes.
Add unsalted butter to the pan and let it melt. Stir in ginger paste, garlic paste, and sugar if using. Cook for about a minute until fragrant and slightly reduced.
Pour in coconut milk into the pan and bring to a simmer. Adjust seasoning with salt as needed. Allow the mixture to cook down by half or until you get your desired consistency.

Stir-fry the nutty-coconut mixture over medium-high heat for about 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking. You can also transfer this step to a wok if you prefer a more traditional stir-fry look and feel.
Remove from heat and immediately serve hot with fresh cilantro on top as a garnish.
